submission.title: Can the Axeman come after you after beating him?
submission.body:
I saw a youtube video [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oVTGLVn\_Reg](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oVTGLVn_Reg) yesterday and was wondering if the axeman can actually come back after you finished the game and if he would be able to get out of his hideout.

Is the guy in the video using a mod, a glitch or anything. Would be super nice if anyone could help me.

comments.body: I saw a couple of videos where it's been a year later,in game time and then go back, and he's running around and wanting to kill you.

I usually come after someone beats me off

aight
does he usually walk around looking for you??? inonly ever saw him in the story missions where he appeared
well thats what im asking too, haaving a boss fight chance to happen or being chqsed over the map would be so fun

I don’t think he does no matter how long you wait I’ve been playing after the game for about 30 maybe 40 days now and he hasn’t come. Also I think I’ve seen posts about this and people said that he doesn’t and that it’s stupid that he doesn’t come to try and kill you
I just realized that I killed him off, but I’ve seen posts about it though.
the guy in the vid clear,y already beat the story but he is also on quest so how does he get him out?
maybe there was an out of bounds glitch wich left gerik alive but completed the mission

submission.title: I hate the fight against gerik
submission.body: I literally hate the fight against gerik, not only the sewer part but also the final fight, because my hands start to tremble and i cant concentrate, but what i hate the most is that i cant behead him when i beat him to keep his head as a trophy

comments.body: Dude you should duel him, it makes it a lot more fun, if you hit him properly with the axe or nail bat, it pushes him, away, does not kill him but is fun,
I should try that out, thanks for the advice

You can make the bombs as he's yapping outside.

Just don't craft in the station in the middle of the room. He stops yapping if you do
I hate it because it’s too easy mostly because I played sns for months and I’ve got real good with guns

it’s stressful! but as the other poster said, do as much work as you can whilst he is monologuing (like a Bond Villian!). The other tip is to keep moving around on a fixed circuit if you backtrack his programming of “just slowly follow the Tourist” will mean you two get dangerously close. 
Give yourself 5-10 seconds at each station and move on regardless of it you have accomplished any thing. Keep going round in the same direction, carrying the bomb you are assembling, even if you fumble with one of the sub tasks/drop something. I was like you and failed the first few times until I did this! Remember there are loads of spare components in those containers at the end wall too. Stay calm! Maybe turn the sound down? Makes it less stressful
Yeah he’s a real tough sonofabitch but he’s got work bench’s for a reason
The whole fight gets 10x easier if you start making the bombs before he comes out to get you. I think that gives you like a free two minutes without you having to do it while being chased.
Sounds like a skill issue.
Guns don't kill Gerik, so what is your point? Really curious 🤔
I find it long Unfun and Repetitive
It gets really fun when he gets pissed off and starts throwing bombs and taking shortcuts to get you
I honestly loved it, the sewer part kept me on edge and the whole time I was waiting for him to sneak me.
true but it is very easy and I'd still of liked more from him
Yea after I play it my headset didn’t work for a while because of all the sweat
Laughs in grenade launcher and explosive arrows, which you technically can’t kill him with but realistically you could stunlock him till he dies of old age
I just wish he was used more
Bro was on a sinner play through
At this point, all of my playthroughs are
Fair

honestly the sewer and the gerik fight is my second favorite part of the game because it fuels my bloodlust (first favorite is the tower raid because the same bloodlust reason but also because the human enemies have guns)
He probably means it’s easy to shoot the explosives. If you can, that is, I just used the grenade launcher to explode the bombs you create.
Or toot his own horn lol!
Could be that too
The fight always glitches out for me. For example i hit im twice with the grenade launcher and then his path finding stopped working. he just yapped and didnt ven try to get me.

submission.title: the most fun one can have in this game

comments.body: lol ..I gotta try that
Definitely trying that later
those records are gone now btw
Lol yes. Send em flying!
When I was in college radio we did that for real, off the 2nd- floor balcony, except Frisbee style, since we didn't have magic punching gloves, definitely fun
Lol

I liked when they rang the bells at the resting place and fought endless waves, but slow enough to manage...I know you can do it anywhere, but you have resources to keep going there.

That was when I discovered that there was a neat little glitch with the Louisiana Kiss gloves.

You know how, if you grab a walker's head with one hand and punch it with the other, it's an instant, splattery decapitation?

That doesn't require you to punch the SAME zombie that you're holding, so you can grab one by the head and kinda hold it off to the side. Then, as the others swarm you, all punches to them are instant decapitations. 

I can't remember if that uses much (if any) stamina, but I think it's certainly less than swinging around a melee weapon. It's SUPER effective in a tight spot, and, besides being a guaranteed kill, it also doesn't require much (again, if any) momentum, so it can be fun to just ball your fist, give them a gentle (Louisiana) kiss, and watch the gore fly.
Or you grab the bat and just hit a bunch of home runs using the skulls as balls

Complete the 3 Exile Trade Network requests for Bourbon Street:
>Bourbon Street (in 2nd story of building behind cocktail lounge)
>  
>Trade1: 3 Rats + 5 medical scissors + 1 egg timer (rwd: Billy bolt cutters)  
>Trade2: Cleaver + Shiv (rwd: lg ammo box)  
>Trade3: 3 National Guard knives + 2 Samdi's Hands + Esteemed Mortal (rwd: 3 lg ammo boxes)  

You will then gain access to the Exile Trade Foothold in the upstairs of the bar which gives you a bunch of energy drinks and ammo for free. You can visit it twice per day, both day and night.

submission.title: Come at me, bro

comments.body: For reference my military is 6 turns away but we have the same size Force. Japan was squaring up and this is the forward guard to hold them off while the rest of the military assembles to meet them in the field

The land look too juicy. Rivers, jungles, deserts and full of hills.

Not to mention, enemies need to cross river to attack you + your citadels can automatically attack.

If it was me, I would declare war right away to farm xp and bring 1-2 workers to build road up there.

Both 2 cities also are juicy, must capture at all cost.

That's the plan This map has a LOOOOOT of land space so there is a wilderness to the East. My main military force is upgrading to Muskets and forming up for a massive flank while the bulk of their military is grinding against Oasis Outpost.


Even if they were to take Oasis (unlikely) it would be an extremely Phyrric victory and my main army would still mop them up.


I had a detachment stationed in Oasis and saw them arranging their forces for an invasion so I set up, and locked in, and fired the first shots

Don't bring workers  , Steal workers 
There is 6 workers in the left, it would be a waste to create a new workers
It's not *slavery,* it's just practical.
[That went well](https://imgur.com/a/h4LIFti)
what difficulty is this on??

How'd it go, I can see you winning this but those mountains and hills in-between Oasis and the forts are going to make it hard to reinforce the forts, how'd you stop the Japanese from going around and attacking from the east? 

What difficulty are you on?

Emperor.
But we have really really really buffed Barbarians (The Barbarians mod) with Raging Barbarians and a huge fuckin land map. It kept every nation dredged down at the start (and they are STILL sniping Settlers. It's the 1700s)

Emperor. I had my military staged south and was trickling reinforcements in from Oasis using the DBZ religion build (holy warriors + Faith per City + just war)


They managed to capture the northern Citadel for one turn but I had two musketeers ready to push them off. This combined with rotating musketeers and pushing more Chinese Bowmen (that 2 attacks per turn is crazy) helped hold the front. The Citadels did their jobs by auto-attacking so I could prioritize damaging units to the point that they would be destroyed by the next turn.


Another factor was positioning my scouts to raid their luxuries and cut off their roads. By turn 2 of the war they were at -33% for every unit, and I enslaved their workers to build roads connecting Oasis down the southern Ridge and the valley.


Bringing guns to the sword fight always helps. 💪

submission.title: What difficulty and speed do yall play on for a fun, strategic and challenging (that is still fun) game?

comments.body: I go Prince Quick, because that's "no advantages, no disadvantages, play them straight up" as a difficulty that concludes usually somewhere before turn 300 or so.
Quick King!

king is just too scuffed for me. It seems for me as if all other countries spawn right next to me even on huge or custom map size. i dont have time to set up before all teh land around me is stolen, then i perish
But it ain't no fun if it is to easy. King is the right ballance for me. With King I don't win every game.
I usually play on large map, inner sea map generation type.

I play medium inner sea; four players (myself included) and a small quantity of city states (usually 6?) gives enough space and fun options to be interesting but not so much as to drastically unbalance anything.

Deity and quick with a high tier civ. I still haven't figured out a way to win with any of the low tier civs so they're not really fun if I'm losing every time.

I lose patience if I don't play on quick, so quick is the most fun for me.

You asked for other people's opinions on what difficulty and speed they play for fun, strategic, and challenging fun, and you respond with your own problems/inability to play.

Everyone has their own preferences and levels for fun.

Oh very close to ideal for me!

I started opting in for barbarians instead of city states because city states placements in the map isn't really random enough. That takes out certain civ options like Greece though.

Both.  I \_also\_ take barbarians.  The two go together and have some neat interactions ("Please kill this barbarian camp near us it's scaring us, there's 50 influence points in it for you" is so valuable).
easy but not too easy so cheiftan for me

There was a time when I solely stcik to Deity runs, but lately i stay Immortal and lower. For speed, anything beyond standard is a slogfest for me. I'll probably never touch marathon until i am old and retired and had nothing else to do in my life.
I like the long game, epic to marathon on usual king or emperor. I like the slow feel, I can play long wars that don't feel like I need to upgrade my troops all the time.
What I personally find fun is seeing my civ develop over time and have more time to develop it's history and to have wars ect like in real life, so I personally like going marathon or epic

Epic, Emperor


Epic makes the battles matter more and you are less-likely to have the enemy bs upgrade half their army in the middle of your war.

Epic and Marathon are what make big and meaningful wars. 


Plus there is less a chance of the opps upgrading their entire military mid-battle.
EPIC GANG

Quick, Prince with some custom map

(I'm kinda new to this game)
